analysis.overall_assessment: The tea leaf shows no visible signs of disease or pollution damage, with vibrant green color and intact structure indicating optimal health. The high confidence score (0.90) confirms robust physiological condition.

immediate_actions: Handle leaves with care to maintain integrity
Transport to processing facility within 2 hours of harvest
processing_recommendations.sorting_strategy: All leaves meet premium quality standards; no sorting required beyond standard size/grade separation
processing_recommendations.processing_method: Traditional orthodox processing with controlled oxidation for premium green tea
quality_preservation: Process within 4 hours of harvest to prevent enzymatic degradation
Use shaded drying methods to preserve chlorophyll

waste_prevention.salvageable_portions: 100% of harvested leaves are suitable for premium processing
waste_prevention.composting_guidelines: Not applicable for healthy leaves; compost only if leaves become unsuitable post-harvest
prevention_measures: Implement regular field monitoring every 72 hours
Maintain buffer zones between tea fields and potential pollution sources
Use organic fertilizers to prevent chemical contamination

cost_saving_opportunities: Optimize harvest timing to maximize yield
Reduce processing waste through precise machinery calibration
Sell premium-grade leaves at 25-30% higher market price
value_recovery_methods: Direct sale as premium loose-leaf tea
Create value-added products like matcha from first-flush leaves

priority_actions: Install real-time air quality monitoring at field edges
Train harvesters in precision plucking techniques
recommendations.timeline: Immediate (0-7 days) for monitoring setup; Ongoing for farming practices
